steps.title: Set the graphs equal
steps.content:
At an intersection, both equations have the same $y$-value.
$$4x^2-23x=x$$

steps.title: Factor the equation
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side and factor.
$$4x^2-24x=0$$
$$4x(x-6)=0$$
So $x=0$ or $x=6$.

steps.title: Use the other intersection
steps.content: The point $(0,0)$ is already given. The other point has coordinates $(a,a)$, so $a$ is the nonzero $x$-value. The answer is 6.
